topic: fraction multiplication word problem, reasoning about operations
latex: Gavin plans to repaint some classroom bookcases. All of the bookcases are the same size and each requires $\frac{13}{16}$ gallon of paint. If he's going to paint $5$ bookcases, how many gallons of paint does he need?
answer_latex: To solve, we'll need to \textbf{multiply 13/16 by 5}, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the \textbf{NUMBER of groups}, but we don't know the \textbf{TOTAL gallons of paint}.
